name: Neo4j (Cluster)
description: Leading native graph database with Cypher query language, ACID transactions, and scalable cluster architecture for connected data

license.comment: Community Edition is GPL-3.0. Enterprise Edition requires commercial license. APOC and other extensions have separate licensing.

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted Community/Enterprise editions. AuraDB is the official managed service. Available on AWS, Azure, and GCP marketplaces.

initial-release.comment: First public release in February 2010. Company founded in 2007, development started earlier.

primary-language.comment: Java (70.1%) with significant Scala (29.2%). The '4j' in Neo4j references Java.

data-model.comment: Native property graph database. Stores nodes, relationships, and properties. Also supports vector embeddings for AI applications.

topology.comment: Cluster uses Primary servers (handle reads/writes via Raft consensus) and Secondary servers (asynchronous replication for read scaling).

sharding.comment: Traditional clustering replicates entire database. Infinigraph (Sept 2025) introduces property sharding - keeps nodes/relationships together, shards properties across machines.

replication-factor.comment: Recommended 3+ primary servers for high availability. 2 primaries possible but not fault-tolerant.

multi-region.comment: Supports multi-data center deployments. Can deploy 3 Core instances per data center across 3 data centers for full DC failure tolerance.

cap-position.comment: CP system - uses ACID model for consistency. Raft consensus ensures consistency in cluster. May reject writes during partitions.

consistency-model.comment: Causal consistency via bookmarks for session-based consistency. ACID transactions provide serializability within transactions.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID compliance. All Cypher queries run within transactions. Transactions are atomic, consistent, isolated, and durable.

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span arbitrary nodes and relationships across the database. Both explicit and implicit transaction modes supported.

isolation-levels.comment: Read-committed isolation by default. Uses multiversion concurrency control (MVCC) for consistent reads.

distributed-transactions.comment: Uses Raft consensus protocol for distributed transaction coordination across primary servers in cluster.

query-language.comment: Cypher is Neo4j's declarative graph query language. ISO GQL standard is based on Cypher.

sql-compatibility.comment: Uses Cypher, not SQL. Cypher is purpose-built for graph traversals with pattern matching syntax (e.g., MATCH, CREATE, MERGE).

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports B-tree, full-text, point (spatial), range, text, and token lookup indexes on node/relationship properties.

joins.comment: Graph traversals replace traditional joins. Native graph storage (adjacency lists) makes multi-hop traversals extremely efficient.

aggregations.comment: Full aggregation support in Cypher: COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, COLLECT, percentile functions, and grouping.

full-text-search.comment: Built-in full-text search indexes using Apache Lucene. Supports fuzzy matching, phrase search, and custom analyzers.

max-cluster-size.comment: No documented maximum cluster size. Typical deployments use 3-9 primary servers with additional read replicas.

write-throughput.comment: Highly workload-dependent. Optimized for graph traversals rather than bulk writes. Vendor benchmarks not directly comparable.

read-latency-p99.comment: Sub-millisecond for bounded (≤2-hop) queries. Complex traversals 14-3100ms depending on query complexity per independent benchmarks.

auto-scaling.comment: Manual scaling by adding/removing servers. AuraDB managed service offers more automated scaling capabilities.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Read replicas can distribute read load. Infinigraph property sharding (2025) improves distribution for large graphs.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported in cluster deployments by upgrading one server at a time.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-optional property graph model. Indexes and constraints can be created online without locking.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Supported via --restore-until parameter on backup restore. Requires Enterprise Edition. Can restore to specific UTC timestamp.

backup-strategy.comment: Full and differential backups (Enterprise). Differential backups form chains for PITR. Supports cloud storage (S3, Azure, GCS).

kubernetes-operator.comment: No official Kubernetes operator. Uses Helm charts for K8s deployment. neo4j-helm provides deployment automation.

rust-driver.comment: neo4rs is the community-maintained Rust driver. Supports Bolt protocol from Neo4j 4.4+.

go-driver.comment: Official Go driver maintained by Neo4j. Part of the officially supported driver suite.

java-driver.comment: Official Java driver using Bolt protocol. Mature driver with reactive support.

dotnet-driver.comment: Official .NET driver maintained by Neo4j. Supports .NET Standard.

python-driver.comment: Official Python driver (neo4j package). Optional Rust extensions available for 10x performance improvement.

ruby-driver.comment: Community neo4j-ruby-driver with pure Ruby and JRuby implementations. API mirrors official Java driver.

c-cpp-driver.comment: libneo4j-omni is a community C driver. Not officially maintained by Neo4j.

orm-support.comment: Spring Data Neo4j for Java. Neo4j OGM (Object-Graph Mapper). No traditional SQL ORMs due to graph model.

open-source.comment: Community Edition is open source under GPL-3.0. Enterprise Edition requires commercial license for production use.

free-tier.comment: AuraDB Free tier available for learning and exploration. Community Edition free for self-hosted deployments.

pricing-model.comment: AuraDB: consumption-based pricing from $65/month (Professional). Enterprise Edition requires license. Self-managed Community is free.

cost-estimate-small.comment: AuraDB Professional starts at $65/month. Business Critical with HA starts at $146/month. 4GB instance ~$259/month.

change-data-capture.comment: Built-in CDC with DIFF and FULL modes. Captures node/relationship creates, updates, deletes. Supports Kafka via official connector.

watch-subscribe.comment: No native client-side subscriptions. CDC is server-side polling via procedures (db.cdc.query). Requires external systems for push notifications.

triggers.comment: APOC triggers allow custom procedures to fire on data changes. Part of APOC extension library, not core database.

event-streaming.comment: Official Neo4j Connector for Kafka (v5.1 GA). Supports CDC strategy for streaming changes to Kafka topics.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: More than 15,000 questions on Stack Overflow per Neo4j documentation.
